run_02_fix.issue: RUN-02: tier 1 grid has 2 empty slots in last row
run_02_fix.cause: tieredValue([6,7,8,8]) yields totalPairs=7 at tier 1. gridRows = Math.ceil(14/4) = 4 (line 61). 4 rows x 4 cols = 16 slots but only 14 tiles are placed (line 145: totalTiles = 7*2 = 14). The last 2 cells in row 4 (indices 14 and 15) have no tile. Clicking them is silently ignored (onClick line 176: clickedIndex===-1 returns early). No crash, no score bug — but visible empty grey area.

grid_arithmetic.tier_0_pairs_6: gridRows = ceil(12/4) = 3 — full 3x4, no empty slots
grid_arithmetic.tier_2_pairs_8: gridRows = ceil(16/4) = 4 — full 4x4, no empty slots
grid_arithmetic.tier_3_pairs_8: gridRows = ceil(16/4) = 4 — full 4x4, no empty slots

run_02_fix.empty_cell_error: No error thrown. onClick findIndex returns -1 for coordinates with no tile; line 176 guard 'if (clickedIndex === -1) return;' silently discards the click.

fix_options.option_A: Change tieredValue([6,7,8,8]) to tieredValue([6,8,8,8]) — tier 1 goes from 7 to 8 pairs. 8*2=16 tiles fills the 4x4 exactly. One-character change, no layout code touched. Slightly harder at tier 1 (1 extra pair) but within acceptable range.
fix_options.option_B: Change tieredValue([6,7,8,8]) to tieredValue([6,6,8,8]) — tier 1 drops to 6 pairs matching tier 0. gridRows=ceil(12/4)=3, full 3x4. Easier at tier 1, bigger difficulty gap before tier 2.
fix_options.option_C: Keep 7 pairs, change gridCols to be derived rather than fixed 4 — e.g. pick gridCols=2 for 6 pairs (2x6), gridCols=4 for 8 pairs. More complex, touches layout.

code_change.rationale: Tier 1 now uses 8 pairs = 16 tiles = full 4x4 grid. Zero empty cells. Consistent with RUNE_SET having exactly 8 entries. The comment on line 45 already says 'RUNE_SET has exactly 8' which supports this value.

score_formula.clamp_correct: timeInside is clamped to totalTime at line 256 via Math.min(this.timeInside + dt, this.totalTime). So timeInside/totalTime <= 1.0 exactly. finalScore can reach exactly 100 only if player is inside for the full totalTime seconds.
score_formula.divisor_analysis: totalTime is the fixed game duration (10/9/8/7s by tier). Using totalTime as divisor is correct — it measures absolute coverage, not relative-to-elapsed. If the player is inside for all totalTime seconds, score=100. No off-by-one.
score_formula.timer_drift: timeLeft decrements via setInterval (wall-clock approximation); elapsedTime accumulates via getDeltaTime() in rAF (frame-accurate). endGame uses timeInside/totalTime — independent of both accumulators. No drift vulnerability in the score formula.

edge_case_never_moves.description: Player never moves cursor — stays at initial position (0,0).
edge_case_never_moves.distance_from_zone: sqrt(190^2 + 190^2) = ~268.7px >> startRadius 120px. isInside=false for entire run.
edge_case_never_moves.verdict: Correct behavior — poor player gets score 0, not a crash or negative score.

hud_vs_score_discrepancy.description: Live HUD and status element display 'timeInside / elapsedTime * 100' (concentration ratio = fraction of time spent inside). endGame scores 'timeInside / totalTime * 100' (absolute coverage). These diverge: e.g. player inside 100% of the first 5s of a 10s game shows HUD=100% but will score 50 at end. Not a bug but potentially confusing to players. Consider aligning HUD to the actual scoring metric.

runes_new_bugs.description: kbFocusIdx initialized to -1 (line 112). First ArrowRight press: kbFocusIdx = Math.min(maxIdx, -1 + 1) = Math.min(maxIdx, 0) = 0. Correct — lands on tile 0. First ArrowLeft: Math.max(0, -1-1) = Math.max(0,-2) = 0. Also correct. First ArrowDown: Math.min(maxIdx, -1+4) = Math.min(maxIdx, 3) = 3 (tile 3 = row 0, col 3). Reasonable. All first-keypress outcomes are valid tile indices. No dead-zone, no out-of-bounds.

Partial-grid keyboard column drift (tier 1 only, 14 tiles). With the current 7-pair config (pending fix RUN-02), ArrowDown from index 10 (row 2, col 2) yields Math.min(13, 14) = 13 (row 3, col 3), not row 3 col 2 (which would be index 14 — a non-existent tile). The nav clamps to the last valid tile index instead of staying in the same column. Effect: keyboard cursor silently skips column when navigating down into the partial last row. Not a crash; not a score bug; but disorienting for keyboard-only players.
runes_new_bugs.fix: Resolved automatically if RUN-02 fix (option_A) is applied: 8 pairs = 16 tiles = full grid, all ArrowDown destinations exist.

runes_new_bugs.description: Score formula: pairScore = (matchedCount / totalPairs) * 70 + timeBonus = Math.max(0, timeLeft/totalTime) * 30. Range [0, 100]. The Math.max(0,...) guard on timeBonus is correct — timeLeft can be marginally negative when endGame fires from the setInterval tick (timeLeft <= 0 triggers endGame). Without the guard, timeBonus could be a small negative value. Guard is present and correct.

Zombie rAF guard: render() line 291 checks 'if (!this.ctx || !this.canvas || this.ended) return'. endGame() sets this.ended=true BEFORE calling cancelAnimationFrame (via cancelTimers). Any rAF that fires after endGame() but before cancelAnimationFrame executes is caught by the guard. No zombie render loop possible.

summary.run_02_status: BUG CONFIRMED — 2 empty slots at tier 1. Fix: change totalPairs tier 1 from 7 to 8.
summary.sang_froid_status: CLEAN — score formula correct, clamp correct, no edge-case bugs.
summary.runes_new_bugs_medium_note: RUN-KB-02 (keyboard column drift in partial grid) — resolved as side-effect of RUN-02 fix.

issues.issue: Tier 1 grid: 7 pairs = 14 tiles in 4x4 = 16 slots. 2 empty cells visible in last row.
issues.recommendation: Change tieredValue([6,7,8,8]) to tieredValue([6,8,8,8]) — tier 1 uses 8 pairs, fills grid completely.

issues.issue: Keyboard ArrowDown navigation drifts to wrong column when navigating into partial last row (tier 1 only).
issues.recommendation: Resolved automatically by applying RUN-02 fix.

issues.issue: Live HUD shows timeInside/elapsedTime (concentration %) while endGame scores timeInside/totalTime (absolute coverage). Diverge after partial play.
issues.recommendation: Consider changing HUD to display timeInside/totalTime*100 to match the scoring metric. Not a scoring bug.

action_items.action: Apply RUN-02 fix: mg_runes.ts line 60 — change 7 to 8 in tieredValue array
Optional: align sang_froid HUD metric to scoring metric (timeInside/totalTime)
